title: Mesosphaerum molle
taxonomy: Mesosphaerum molle is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is often classified as a member of the genus Mesosphaerum, which is part of the larger clade of mint family plants. Mesosphaerum molle is closely related to other species in the same genus, such as Mesosphaerum suaveolens. Further classification may be available through more detailed botanical studies.
uses: Mesosphaerum molle is a plant species used in traditional medicine. The plant is valued for its expectorant properties, used to treat respiratory issues. In some cultures, Mesosphaerum molle is also used to treat fever, coughs, and skin conditions. Its leaves and stems are often used to make infusions or teas for medicinal purposes. More research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and its effectiveness in treating various health conditions.

intro: Mesosphaerum molle, also known as Plectranthus amboinicus, is a perennial herb belonging to the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its soft, velvety leaves and stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Mesosphaerum molle include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Mesosphaerum molle is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional Indian and Mediterranean cooking, where it is valued for its culinary and medicinal uses.
